bull.b: High and growing Gold adoption and strong deposit inflows create durable recurring revenue and cross-sell leverage. A larger base of paid subscribers and sticky deposits supports stable net interest and subscription revenue even if trading volumes fluctuate, improving long-term revenue mix resiliency.

Sustained high adjusted EBITDA margins, coupled with AI-driven productivity gains, indicate scalable unit economics. Margin strength provides buffer for reinvestment and buybacks while enabling profitable growth as product adoption scales, supporting durable earnings power over multiple quarters.

Consistent, large net deposit inflows strengthen liquidity and fund interest-earning assets and margin lending growth. Improved operating and free cash flow historically coupled with deposit growth enhances funding stability and reduces reliance on external capital for growth initiatives.

bear.b: Higher debt-to-equity constrains financial flexibility and increases sensitivity to interest rates or an earnings setback. In a downturn or revenue slowdown, elevated leverage could force cost cutting or limit investment optionality, making capital allocation decisions more constrained over the medium term.

Material recent TTM revenue decline highlights dependence on cyclical trading and monetization mix. Even with margin improvement, sustained revenue pressure would limit reinvestment and could expose operating leverage; durable growth requires re-accelerating non-transaction streams.

Regulatory uncertainty for prediction markets and complex jurisdictional issues create execution risk for a key growth vector. Delays or restrictions could limit scaling and monetization of novel products, reducing expected revenue diversification and exposing results to legal and compliance costs over time.
